mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.5 percent, labor market slack triggers recession fears that compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 forward returns within the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signal broad-based consumer and builder confidence that sustains corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a single-series baseline, or 60-day forward returns are negative in over 60 percent of co-rising instances, rejecting the confidence transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during an early expansion phase reflects capacity utilization improvements that boost near-term earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in 70 percent of months where INDPRO accelerates during early expansion, or the mechanism shows zero incremental information over monetary policy signals.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year CPI increase exceeding 2.5 percent triggers inflation risk premia and expectations of central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and forces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50 percent of high-inflation months, or the inflation threshold shows no statistical relationship with equity drawdowns (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 20-day returns in over 65 percent of triggered months, or the interaction term loses significance (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth rates reflect expanding aggregate demand that directly translates into higher corporate profits, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 changes show a Rank IC below 0.01 over any 5-year rolling window, or 60-day equity returns are uncorrelated with GDP growth across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing industrial production and rising consumer prices indicates supply-side bottlenecks that erode profit margins, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following week during stagflationary periods.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of divergence instances, or the margin compression channel fails to manifest in earnings revision data during high-inflation/low-growth quarters.
